parts.text: Single task: review `git diff HEAD` for potential bugs/regressions in current changes only. Return a concise markdown list of actionable issues. If none, return `- No critical issues found.`

input.description: Shows all current changes against HEAD

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/registry.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"io"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
9: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
10: )
11: 
12: // ExecContext holds the minimal context needed by generic tool executors.
13: type ExecContext struct {
14: 	Workdir string
15: 	Stderr  io.Writer
16: 	Verbose bool
17: }
18: 
19: // ToolEntry holds a tool's definition and executor functions.
20: type ToolEntry struct {
21: 	Definition func() provider.Tool
22: 	Execute    func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult
23: }
24: 
25: // Registry maps tool names to their definitions and executors.
26: // Registration must happen at startup before any concurrent Dispatch calls.
27: // Concurrent Dispatch calls (read-only) are safe after registration is complete.
28: type Registry struct {
29: 	entries map[string]ToolEntry
30: }
31: 
32: // NewRegistry returns a new Registry with an empty, initialized entries map.
33: func NewRegistry() *Registry {
34: 	return &Registry{
35: 		entries: make(map[string]ToolEntry),
36: 	}
37: }
38: 
39: // Register adds a tool entry to the registry, keyed by name.
40: // If a tool with the same name already exists, it is silently replaced.
41: func (r *Registry) Register(name string, entry ToolEntry) {
42: 	r.entries[name] = entry
43: }
44: 
45: // Has returns true if a tool with the given name exists in the registry.
46: func (r *Registry) Has(name string) bool {
47: 	_, ok := r.entries[name]
48: 	return ok
49: }
50: 
51: // Resolve takes a list of tool names and returns their provider.Tool definitions.
52: // Each entry's Definition() is called on every Resolve invocation (not cached).
53: // Returns an error if any name is not found in the registry or has a nil Definition.
54: // Returns an empty non-nil slice for nil or empty input.
55: func (r *Registry) Resolve(names []string) ([]provider.Tool, error) {
56: 	if len(names) == 0 {
57: 		return []provider.Tool{}, nil
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	tools := make([]provider.Tool, 0, len(names))
61: 	for _, name := range names {
62: 		entry, ok := r.entries[name]
63: 		if !ok {
64: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q", name)
65: 		}
66: 		if entry.Definition == nil {
67: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("tool %q has no definition", name)
68: 		}
69: 		tools = append(tools, entry.Definition())
70: 	}
71: 	return tools, nil
72: }
73: 
74: // Dispatch looks up the tool by call.Name and executes it.
75: // Returns (ToolResult, nil) on success, or (zero ToolResult, error) for
76: // unknown tools or tools with nil executors.
77: func (r *Registry) Dispatch(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (provider.ToolResult, error) {
78: 	entry, ok := r.entries[call.Name]
79: 	if !ok {
80: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q", call.Name)
81: 	}
82: 	if entry.Execute == nil {
83: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("tool %q has no executor", call.Name)
84: 	}
85: 	return entry.Execute(ctx, call, ec), nil
86: }
87: 
88: // RegisterAll registers all implemented tool entries into the given registry.
89: // Both cmd/run.go and ExecuteCallAgent call this after NewRegistry().
90: // Future milestones add lines here — no call-site changes needed.
91: // Safe to call multiple times (Register silently replaces duplicates).
92: func RegisterAll(r *Registry) {
93: 	r.Register(toolname.ListDirectory, listDirectoryEntry())
94: 	r.Register(toolname.ReadFile, readFileEntry())
95: 	r.Register(toolname.WriteFile, writeFileEntry())
96: 	r.Register(toolname.EditFile, editFileEntry())
97: 	r.Register(toolname.RunCommand, runCommandEntry())
98: 	r.Register(toolname.URLFetch, urlFetchEntry())
99: }

(End of file - total 99 lines)
</content>

<content>1: package agent
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"errors"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"path/filepath"
8: 	"strings"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/BurntSushi/toml"
11: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
12: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/xdg"
13: )
14: 
15: // MemoryConfig holds memory sub-configuration for an agent.
16: type MemoryConfig struct {
17: 	Enabled    bool   `toml:"enabled"`
18: 	Path       string `toml:"path"`
19: 	LastN      int    `toml:"last_n"`
20: 	MaxEntries int    `toml:"max_entries"`
21: }
22: 
23: // ParamsConfig holds model parameter overrides for an agent.
24: type ParamsConfig struct {
25: 	Temperature float64 `toml:"temperature"`
26: 	MaxTokens   int     `toml:"max_tokens"`
27: }
28: 
29: // SubAgentsConfig holds sub-agent execution configuration for an agent.
30: type SubAgentsConfig struct {
31: 	MaxDepth int   `toml:"max_depth"`
32: 	Parallel *bool `toml:"parallel"`
33: 	Timeout  int   `toml:"timeout"`
34: }
35: 
36: // AgentConfig represents a parsed agent TOML configuration file.
37: type AgentConfig struct {
38: 	Name          string          `toml:"name"`
39: 	Description   string          `toml:"description"`
40: 	Model         string          `toml:"model"`
41: 	SystemPrompt  string          `toml:"system_prompt"`
42: 	Skill         string          `toml:"skill"`
43: 	Files         []string        `toml:"files"`
44: 	Workdir       string          `toml:"workdir"`
45: 	Tools         []string        `toml:"tools"`
46: 	SubAgents     []string        `toml:"sub_agents"`
47: 	SubAgentsConf SubAgentsConfig `toml:"sub_agents_config"`
48: 	Memory        MemoryConfig    `toml:"memory"`
49: 	Params        ParamsConfig    `toml:"params"`
50: }
51: 
52: // Validate checks that required fields are present in the agent configuration.
53: // It checks name first (fail-fast): if name is missing, it returns that error
54: // without checking model.
55: func Validate(cfg *AgentConfig) error {
56: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.Name) == "" {
57: 		return errors.New("agent config missing required field: name")
58: 	}
59: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.Model) == "" {
60: 		return errors.New("agent config missing required field: model")
61: 	}
62: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth < 0 {
63: 		return errors.New("sub_agents_config.max_depth must be non-negative")
64: 	}
65: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth > 5 {
66: 		return errors.New("sub_agents_config.max_depth cannot exceed 5")
67: 	}
68: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.Timeout < 0 {
69: 		return errors.New("sub_agents_config.timeout must be non-negative")
70: 	}
71: 	if cfg.Memory.LastN < 0 {
72: 		return errors.New("memory.last_n must be non-negative")
73: 	}
74: 	if cfg.Memory.MaxEntries < 0 {
75: 		return errors.New("memory.max_entries must be non-negative")
76: 	}
77: 	validTools := toolname.ValidNames()
78: 	for _, name := range cfg.Tools {
79: 		if !validTools[name] {
80: 			return fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q in tools config", name)
81: 		}
82: 	}
83: 	return nil
84: }
85: 
86: // Load reads and parses an agent TOML configuration file by name.
87: // The name parameter is the agent name without the .toml extension.
88: func Load(name string) (*AgentConfig, error) {
89: 	configDir, err := xdg.GetConfigDir()
90: 	if err != nil {
91: 		return nil, err
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	path := filepath.Join(configDir, "agents", name+".toml")
95: 
96: 	if _, err := os.Stat(path); os.IsNotExist(err) {
97: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent config not found: %s", name)
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
101: 	if err != nil {
102: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read agent config %q: %w", name, err)
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	var cfg AgentConfig
106: 	if _, err := toml.Decode(string(data), &cfg); err != nil {
107: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse agent config %q: %w", name, err)
108: 	}
109: 
110: 	if err := Validate(&cfg); err != nil {
111: 		return nil, err
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	return &cfg, nil
115: }
116: 
117: // List returns all valid agent configurations from the agents directory.
118: // Invalid files are silently skipped. If the agents directory does not exist,
119: // an empty slice is returned.
120: func List() ([]AgentConfig, error) {
121: 	configDir, err := xdg.GetConfigDir()
122: 	if err != nil {
123: 		return nil, err
124: 	}
125: 
126: 	agentsDir := filepath.Join(configDir, "agents")
127: 
128: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(agentsDir)
129: 	if err != nil {
130: 		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
131: 			return []AgentConfig{}, nil
132: 		}
133: 		return nil, err
134: 	}
135: 
136: 	var agents []AgentConfig
137: 	for _, entry := range entries {
138: 		if entry.IsDir() {
139: 			continue
140: 		}
141: 		if !strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".toml") {
142: 			continue
143: 		}
144: 		name := strings.TrimSuffix(entry.Name(), ".toml")
145: 		cfg, err := Load(name)
146: 		if err != nil {
147: 			continue // skip invalid files
148: 		}
149: 		agents = append(agents, *cfg)
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	return agents, nil
153: }
154: 
155: // Scaffold returns a TOML template string for a new agent configuration.
156: // The name argument is interpolated into the template.
157: func Scaffold(name string) (string, error) {
158: 	tmpl := `name = "` + name + `"
159: description = ""
160: 
161: # Full provider/model per models.dev
162: model = "provider/model-name"
163: 
164: # Agent persona (optional)
165: # system_prompt = ""
166: 
167: # Default skill (optional, can be overridden with --skill flag)
168: # skill = ""
169: 
170: # Context files - glob patterns resolved from workdir or cwd (optional)

</content>

parts.text:
- **High:** `web_search` is now accepted by config validation (`internal/toolname/toolname.go:28`), but it is not registered in `RegisterAll` (`internal/tool/registry.go:92`), so agents that specify `tools = ["web_search"]` will validate successfully and then fail at runtime with `unknown tool "web_search"` during tool resolution (`internal/tool/tool.go:241`).  
  **Action:** either register/implement `web_search` in `internal/tool/registry.go` in the same change, or remove it from `ValidNames()` until implementation lands.
